entries.definition:
in his right hand; *(2:101) الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ (epithet for the Jews in
particular and also for the Christians) those who were given the
Scripture 2 to be granted something (20:36) قَالَ قَدْ أُوتِيتَ سُؤْلَكَ يَامُوسَى
God said, ‘Moses, you have been granted your request.’

آتِي ʾātī [act. part., fem. آتِيَة ʾātiyatun] 1 someone who or
something which is coming (15:85) وَإِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ and the Hour is
certainly coming 2 someone who or something which comes
(44:19) إِنِّي آتِيكُم بِسُلْطَانٍ مُّبِينٍ I come to you with clear authority.

إِيتَاء ʾītāʾ [v. n.] 1 the act of giving (16:90) إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ
... وَالإِحْسَانِ وَإِيتَاءِ ذِي الْقُرْبَى God commands justice doing good, and
giving to relatives ... 2 paying (24:37) رِجَالٌ لاَّ تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجَارَةٌ وَلاَ بَيْعٌ عَن
ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَإِقَامِ الصَّلاةِ وَإِيتَاءِ الزَّكَاةِ people who are not diverted, either by
commerce or profit, from remembering God, upholding the
prayer and paying the prescribed alms.

مَأْتِيّ maʾtiyy [pass. part.] that which is fulfilled, carried out
(19:61) إِنَّهُ كَانَ وَعْدُهُ مَأْتِيًّا truly His promise will always be fulfilled.

مُؤْتُون muʾtūn [pl. of act. part. مُؤْتِي muʾtī] those who give, grant,
pay up (4:162) وَالْمُقِيمِينَ الصَّلاةَ وَالْمُؤْتُونَ الزَّكَاةَ those who perform the
prayers and pay up prescribed alms.

entries.definition:
ʾ–th–th abundance, great wealth; (of hair and tree branches) to
be thick, intertwined; to be fleshy; furnishings. Of this root, أَثَاث
ʾathāth occurs twice in the Qur’an.

أَثَاث ʾathāth [coll. n.] 1 furnishings (16:80) وَمِنْ أَصْوَافِهَا وَأَوْبَارِهَا
وَأَشْعَارِهَا أَثَاثًا وَمَتَاعًا إِلَى حِينٍ and from their wool, their fur and their
hair, [they have] furnishings and [other] uses/enjoyments for a
while 2 property, wealth (19:74) وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَحْسَنُ أَثَاثًا
وَرِئْيًا how many a generation before them have We destroyed; who
surpassed them in riches and outward splendour.
